Off Calendar Feb-17-2022 Continued To Sep-12-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Feb-17-2022 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-03-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied. There exists a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant had constructive notice of the defect and is liable for the alleged dangerous condition of public property under Gov. Code sec. 835.

Whether defendant had constructive notice of the defect for a sufficient period of time to create liability is a factual issue. (See Gov. Code sec. 835.2(b); Ortega v. Kmart Corp. (2001) 26 Cal.4th 1200, 1207 ["Whether a dangerous condition has existed long enough for a reasonably prudent person to have discovered it is a question of fact for the jury"].) The court liberally construes the evidence in favor of the party opposing summary judgment "and resolve[s] doubts about the propriety of granting the motion in favor of the party opposing it." (Szadolci v. Hollywood Park Operating Co. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 16, 19.)

Here, a reasonable trier of fact could conclude that defendant had constructive notice of the defect. The accident occurred in 2018 and defendant had not inspected the area where the accident occurred since 2010. (Lennon Decl., par. 10.) "We conclude that a plaintiff may prove a dangerous condition existed for an unreasonable time with circumstantial evidence 'evidence that an inspection had not been made within a particular period of time prior to an accident may warrant an inference that the defective condition existed long enough so that a person exercising reasonable care would have discovered it.'" (Ortega, 26 Cal.4th at 1210.) In addition, a reasonable trier of fact could conclude that the hole that plaintiff tripped over in 2018 was the result of the guy wire anchor installation work that occurred in 2014 based on the proximity of the hole to the guy wire anchor. (Rollan Decl., Ex. A [Plaintiff's Depo., 46:6-47:17, 79:18-81:4], Ex. C [pictures]; Larsen Decl., Exs. B and C.) The trier of fact could find constructive notice based on the 4-year time period.

Defendant correctly notes (and plaintiff concedes) that the common law negligence cause of action fails against defendant (public entity) as a matter of law. Defendant is only liable if there is a statutory basis. (See Gov. Code sec. 815(a).) Defendant, however, failed to seek summary adjudication of that claim in the alternative.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
